property_address: 4505 Main St (Part of a 9 Property Portfolio)
transaction_notes:
AH Realty Trust (formerly known as Armada Hoffler) sold a portfolio of 9 multifamily properties located in Georgia, Maryland, North Carolina, and Virginia to Harbor Group International for $485 million, or about $208,155 per unit. 

Two additional properties were under contract at the time of publication and are expected to close by the end of 2026 and by mid-2027, respectively. (See CoStar PID #s 10165133 and 10554155).

The portfolio has a total of 2330 units. 

In February 2026, Armada Hoffler announced its plans to launch the company under a new name, AH Realty Trust. The seller also announced its intentions to invest in and expand its retail real estate portfolio going forward, given the segment's durable cash flow profile and growth potential. The seller also announced its plans to sell 11 of the 14 multi-family properties in its portfolio at that time.

The seller intends to use the sale proceeds to reduce its debt as it progresses towards a long-term leverage target of 5.5x-6x
description_text:
This multifamily asset at Town Center of Virginia Beach is one component of a larger mixed-use development that was completed in 2014 as part of the ongoing, public-private partnership between the City of Virginia Beach and Armada Hoffler Properties. The mixed-use development includes 286 upscale apartments and an extensive array of amenities at Encore 4505 as well as a 15-story LEED Silver certified office tower, approximately 950 parking spaces in a structured garage, and 23,000 square feet of retail which is 100% leased. In addition to the benefits that come with living in the Town Center of Virginia Beach, Encore 4505 includes a two-story clubhouse with pool tables, four resort-inspired courtyards, refreshing swimming pool with sundeck, comfortably furnished lounge areas with fire pits, a state-of-the-art fitness center, business center, and meeting space.

The bed-bath mix, unit counts and sizes are confirmed per property management.

description_text:
This multifamily asset features nearly 200 units with a spacious community room, resort-style pool and 24-hour fitness center. Liberty Apartments is the residential component of a larger $72 million mixed-use development project that was born out of a public-private partnership between the City of Newport News, the Commonwealth of Virginia and Armada Hoffler Properties. In addition to Liberty Apartments, the mixed-use development included an 81,000 square-foot complex, home to Newport News Shipbuilding’s Apprentice School, as well as street-level retail space and a 380-car parking garage on approximately 6 acres of land.

The bed-bath mix, unit counts and sizes are estimated.

transaction_notes: A private investor sold this 8-unit multifamily building to a private investor for $960,000, or $120,000 per unit. The building was fully leased at the time of sale. The property was on the market for 70 days, with an initial asking price of $1,035,000. At the time of sale, the brick building featured a new roof and 2-bedroom, 1-bathroom units. The buyer secured financing of $300,000. The property's prime location near the interstate, shipyards, and employment centers keeps demand strong and vacancies low. The information in this comparable has been verified by the listing broker and the buyer's broker.

A private individual sold this 96-unit multifamily property to PRG Real Estate Management, Inc. for $19,500,000, or $203,125 per unit. The property was approximately 97.92% occupied at the time of sale.

Built in 1989, Herons Point Apartments consists of five two-story buildings totaling 53,456 square feet. The garden-style apartment community sits directly adjacent to The Courtyards of Chanticleer, a 304-unit community in the PRG portfolio. The proximity of the two properties allows PRG to streamline operations, improve efficiencies, and leverage shared resources while maintaining a strong focus on delivering a high-quality resident experience.

The buyers were drawn to the property's combination of location, waterfront setting, and operational upside, with plans to make targeted improvements that elevate the community while maintaining its established character. 

The buyer financed the acquisition with a loan of $12,675,000.

Known as MacDonald Manor, the property is a 152-unit affordable housing community. The property was previously subject to the Federal Low-Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC) program, serving families with incomes at or below 60% of the Area Median Income (AMI) prior to this transaction.

property_address: 9616 1st Bay St (Part of a 13 Property Sale)
transaction_notes:
A private individual sold a portfolio of thirteen multi-family properties in Norfolk, VA to Triton Property Management for $7.55 million, or about $123,770 per unit.

The portfolio has a total of 71 units. (Public records shows a net rentable square footage of 52,486 square feet while a contact for the listing broker noted the net rentable square footage of 53,250 square feet)

The property was sold in an off market transaction.

Overall occupancy of the portfolio was about 85% at the time of the sale.

The seller was part of a partnership looking to pursue other opportunities.

The buyer acquired the property as a value add opportunity as the properties were constructed between in the 1960s and 1980s and most of the units were largely under-renovated.

There were no conditions which affected the sale price. Despite the properties being vintage apartments, there were only minor deferred maintenance issues which were not significant.

The in place cap rate was reported to be about 6%,

description_text: Marsh Landing is a 250-unit apartment community located in themidtown area of Portsmouth, Virginia. This property was originally constructed in 1942 and renovated in 1999 under the Section 42 Low Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC) program. The purchaser will have the ability to place new financing on the property. Marsh Landing consists of all two bedroom-one bath apartment homes with 156 garden style apartment homes and 94 town home units. Residents enjoy a variety of value-added features including the community club house, children’s playground and on-site laundry facilities. Units are well-equipped with stackable washers and dryer connections and eat-in kitchens. The town home units contain a separate dining room, private entrances and ample storage space. Marsh Landing is located on George Washington Highway in Portsmouth overlooking the marsh known as Paradise Creek. Residents enjoy convenient access to shopping, schools, public transportation and other neighborhood services. The

Independence Realty Trust and Steadfast Apartment REIT announced they completed their merger on December 16.

This was an all-stock sale with each Steadfast share trading for .905 shares in Independence Realty Trust. This equals $2.325 billion based on the $23.32 per share price on December 16 for the 110 million shares of Steadfast Apartment REIT, plus the REIT's debt for a total enterprise value of  $4.425 billion.

On annualized NOI of $198 million as of Q2 2021, this equates to a 4.47% cap rate for the portfolio.

No deeds were filed for the transaction; the combined REIT owns all of the assets, including the entities on title.

On December 1, 2020, Ritz Banc Group (RBG) completed the acquisition of the Lake Village Apartments in Chesapeake, VA for $19.3 million, or $104,891 per unit. 

The 184-unit garden-style community is located at 908 Lake Village Drive in the Hampton Roads market. It features a mix of two- and three-bedroom units and was around 98% leased at the time of the sale.

description_text: The Baylor Apartments, 750 W. Princess Anne Road, Norfolk, Virginia, are located in Norfolk’s Ghent section, one of the strongest rental markets in the Hampton Roads region. The 22 units are comprised of one, two and three bedroom apartments and are in walking distance to Ghent’s famed Colley Avenue, host of several eclectic restaurants and shops. The building sits on the hard corner of Princess Anne Road and Colley Avenue, offering tremendous visibility. In addition to its charming historic homes and apartments, Ghent is in close proximity to major employment centers including Sentara Norfolk General Hospital, Downtown Norfolk, Old Dominion University and the Norfolk Naval Base.

Area Description:
Kecoughthan Road has long played an important role in the development of the City of Hampton. It is  one of the primary east-west transportation links between the downtown districts of both Hampton and Newport News. Easy access fot I-664 making it centrally location creates great access to both peninsula and south side. Because of the importance of the Kecoughtan corridor the city of Hampton embarked on an important master planning posses which was completed January 2006. These recommendations will guide the future development and redevelopment.

potential. With improved management, the Buyer can increase annual occupancy to 95%, the current market average in
the Ocean View submarket. To achieve this occupancy level, the Buyer needs to perform moderate renovations to three
down-units at an estimated cost of $12,000 per unit. With a $625,000 down payment and $36,000 in capital
expenditure, this investment would yield an 17.2% cash-on-cash return for the Buyer within a year.

The property is unique in the market due to its location and amenities. With an eight boat dock along the navigable
Little Creek, residents can boat into the Chesapeake Bay. Residents can enjoy the serene environment along the
property's pier, or head two blocks north to the shores of Norfolk's Chesapeake Bay beaches. The subject property also
offers in-unit washer and dryer connections, a convenient yet uncommon amenity in the submarket. Over

description_text:
Located in the heart of beautiful downtown Hampton, Virginia, Kings Way Apartments blends authentic historic architecture with modern city living. If you would love to experience the hustle and bustle of the fastest growing city in the region, yet desire a quiet, quaint domain, then Kings Way Apartments is the perfect home to lay out your welcome mat! Professionals from many career paths have made Kings Way their home. They especially love all the great little restaurants, shopping & nightlife that make up their neighborhood, and most are within walking distance. Hampton’s quality of life is a breath of fresh air and one of the best kept secrets on the east coast.

Hampton has a distinct small-town feel with little traffic and an abundance of amenities. With an average commute time of 20 minutes, Hampton is literally in the heart of Hampton Roads. A beautiful city with a mild climate, and cost of living well below the national average, Hampton has one of the lowest crime rates in the

description_text: 1215-1217 Colonial Avenue, Norfolk, Virginia, are located in Norfolk’s Ghent section, one of the strongest rental markets in the Hampton Roads MSA (1.6 million). There are two separate buildings. 1215 Colonial Avenue contains six apartments and 1217 contains twenty-four units (see next page for description). Both buildings have significant deferred maintenance. However there is tremendous upside due to the high demand for rental units in this location. The units sit across the street from a brand new Harris Teeter supermarket and are in walking or short driving distance to many eclectic shops and restaurants. In addition to its charming historic homes and apartments, Ghent is in close proximity to major employment centers including Sentara Norfolk General Hospital, Downtown Norfolk, Old Dominion University and the Norfolk Naval Base.

The $704,000 deposit included a $75,000 earnest payment. Corevest American funded a loan of $3,800,000 which will be used by the buyer to convert the current apartments into condos. There are plans to create a common outdoor living area, to include grills and fire pits for residents. The buyer also has unconfirmed plans to expand upon the existing dock which houses 14 boat slips.

description_text:
Pretty Lake Landing Apartments offers potential Buyers the opportunity to purchase a cash flowing asset with upside
potential. With improved management, the Buyer can increase annual occupancy to 95%, the current market average in
the Ocean View submarket. To achieve this occupancy level, the Buyer needs to perform moderate renovations to three
down-units at an estimated cost of $12,000 per unit. With a $625,000 down payment and $36,000 in capital
expenditure, this investment would yield an 17.2% cash-on-cash return for the Buyer within a year.

The property is unique in the market due to its location and amenities. With an eight boat dock along the navigable
Little Creek, residents can boat into the Chesapeake Bay. Residents can enjoy the serene environment along the
property's pier, or head two blocks north to the shores of Norfolk's Chesapeake Bay beaches. The subject property also
offers in-unit washer and dryer connections, a convenient yet uncommon amenity in the submarket. Over

On January 10, 2019, a joint venture Croatan Investments and JSB Capital Group purchased Park Crescent Apartments from Gumenick Properties. Located in Norfolk, Virginia, the 400-unit community was built in two phases. The first phase of 288 units was originally constructed in 1991 by Gumenick and has mostly original interior finishes. The second phase of 112 units includes new Class A amenities featuring a resort-style saltwater pool, clubhouse, and a large fitness center constructed in 2012. Croatan intends to perform a value-add renovation to phase one unit interiors.

Centrally located in Virginia’s second largest city, Park Crescent is in close proximity to I-64, Norfolk International Airport, and Naval Station Norfolk, the world’s largest Naval Base. transaction_notes:
On September 28th, 2018 the24,000 multi-family property at 1415-1419 Lois Ln was sold for $1,800,000 or $60,000 per unit. The property was built in 1988 and zoned R-12. At time of sale it was being used as apartments. This was an investment sale.

The seller was represented by Justin Ferguson and Altay Uzun of Marcus & Millichap. The buyer was represented by Marie Depedro of World Class Realty.

The property was on the market for 4  months, with an initial asking price of $2,070,000.  The transaction was in escrow for approximately 3 months. The actual cap rate at the time of the sale was 7.58%. There was financing through Union Bank on the buyer's side. 

The seller was motivated to divest the property to retire. The buyer was attracted to the property to build their portfolio.

The details of the comp were verified with both the listing and buying brokers.
description_text:
Marcus and Millichap is pleased to present this class C 30 unit
apartment community located at 1417-1419 Lois Lane in Norfolk, VA.
This community is located a couple of blocks from the Norview Ave.
exit off of Interstate 64, and proximate to the Norfolk International
Airport, Naval/Military bases, Shipyards, shopping, and public
transportation.
Investors will see value in the acquisition of this community given the
optimal location, little deferred maintenance, history of high occupancy,
strong underlying economic fundamentals, one of the lowest
multifamily vacancy rates in the country, close proximity to the largest
military base in the world as well as realizing a stable cash flow stream.
Upside lies in filling the few current vacancies as well as rent growth to
match current market rents of 750 per unit.
Located off of the Norview Ave / Norfolk International Airport exit, 2 blocks from I-64

description_text:
Marcus & Millichap is pleased to offer this 10 unit
multifamily investment located at 846 Little Bay Avenue,
Norfolk, Virginia.
The subject property is located in the Willoughby Spit area
of Norfolk. The property is less than one mile from I -64 (last
exit before reaching the Hampton Roads Bridge Tunnel),
less than three miles from Norfolk Naval Station (world's
largest military installation), as well being located on the
same corridor as NAB Little Creek (Naval Amphibious
Base). The property also benefits from being located
across the street from Willoughby Park, and two blocks from
Chesapeake Bay beaches.
Investors will see value in the acquisition of this property
due to the prime location, strong underlying market
fundamentals fueled by the military presence, well
maintained, and upside with raising rents to match market
rents and filling current vacancies.
Located in the Willoughby Spit section of Norfolk, VA.

Located in the heart of beautiful downtown Hampton, Virginia, Kings Way Apartments blends authentic historic architecture with modern city living. If you would love to experience the hustle and bustle of the fastest growing city in the region, yet desire a quiet, quaint domain, then Kings Way Apartments is the perfect home to lay out your welcome mat! Professionals from many career paths have made Kings Way their home. They especially love all the great little restaurants, shopping & nightlife that make up their neighborhood, and most are within walking distance. Hampton’s quality of life is a breath of fresh air and one of the best kept secrets on the east coast.

Hampton has a distinct small-town feel with little traffic and an abundance of amenities. With an average commute time of 20 minutes, Hampton is literally in the heart of Hampton Roads. A beautiful city with a mild climate, and cost of living well below the national average, Hampton has one of the lowest crime rates in the

property_address: 870 Lucas Creek Rd (Part of a 3 Property Portfolio)
transaction_notes:
A portfolio of 3 apartment communities in Newport News were sold on September 6, 2018 for $27.91 million.  The sale included:

* Waypoint at Oyster Point, a 100 unit property located at 1 Guenevere Court, sold for $7.75 million at a 5.33% cap rate based on the trailing 12 months net operating income.
* Waypoint at Uptown, a 140 unit property located at 870 Lucas Creek Road, sold for $8.66 million at a 5.32% cap rate.
* Waypoint at Hidenwood, a 104 unit property located at 84 Tall Pines Way, sold for $11.5 million at a 5.88% cap rate.

The blended cap rate based on the trailing 12 months net operating income was 5.55%.  This is a value-add acquisition for the buyer.  Of the 3 properties included, Waypoint at Hidenwood had more upgrades done prior to the sale.

The contract period was about 75 days and the buyer obtained financing but the details were not known.

On September 6th, 2018, the 11 unit apartment building located at 747-749 W Princess Anne Rd in Norfolk, VA was sold for $1,600,000. The property consisted of the following:

-Four 1 BR/1 BA units
-Three 2 BR/1 BA units
-Two 3 BR/1 BA units
-One 3 BR/2 BA unit
-One 4 BR/2 BA unit
-6,718 SF restaurant
-0.19 AC land

This information was confirmed with the listing broker and buyer.

transaction_notes:
Lochaven on the Lake Apartments, a 144-unit apartment community located at 99 Lochaven Drive in Newport News, was sold on August 24, 2018 for $12.857 million.  The property was about 90% occupied at sale.

A party to the transaction verified some of the details of the transaction.  The seller had made about $4 million in capital improvements over the past 3 years with about 105 of the units having been updated.  It is believed the buyer will continue updating the remaining units.

The cap rate based on the trailing income was reported as 7.0%, the net operating income is estimated at $899,990.

Timbers Townhomes, a 104-unit apartment community at 7004 Northgate Drive in Norfolk, was sold on August 8, 2018 for $7.12 million.  The transaction was completed through the transfer of the membership interests in Timbers Norfolk, LLC, a deed will not be recorded in connection with this transaction.

A party to the transaction confirmed the price was $7.12 million.  The property was about 80% occupied at sale, primarily because there were 13 units down.  The estimated cost to bring them back online is about $120,000.  With the down units, there wasn't much of a net income to report.  The trailing income was not disclosed.  With the 13 units back online with a stabilized occupancy the net operating income was estimated by a party to the transaction as about $600,000.

The buyer obtained Fannie Mae financing in the amount of $10.7 million through Barings Multifamily Capital.  It was said that they used an out of state appraiser and the $13.375 million appraisal is higher than market va

On June 6th, 2019, the 32,175 square foot 33 unit Class C Multi-family Building at 2107-2111 Pretty Lake Ave in Norfolk, VA was sold for $3,114,000 or $94,364 per unit. The property is zoned R-12 and was built in 1989. The building was purchased for a reposition investment and will be undergoing condo conversion and redevelopment. The property located north of the building parcel was also included in the sale for $125,000. The structure is the is the only multi-family building in the area that has private dock access. The dock consists of 15 boat slips that will have access to power and water after the redevelopment. 

The sale was originally listed for $3,089,000 or $93,606 per unit. A $25,000 extension was paid to lengthen the escrow length to nine months or 270 days.

The seller was motivated to divest the property to reposition their portfolio. The buyer purchased the property for the location, investment opportunity, and for the amenities the structure offers.

description_text:
Pretty Lake Landing Apartments offers potential Buyers the opportunity to purchase a cash flowing asset with upside
potential. With improved management, the Buyer can increase annual occupancy to 95%, the current market average in
the Ocean View submarket. To achieve this occupancy level, the Buyer needs to perform moderate renovations to three
down-units at an estimated cost of $12,000 per unit. With a $625,000 down payment and $36,000 in capital
expenditure, this investment would yield an 17.2% cash-on-cash return for the Buyer within a year.

The property is unique in the market due to its location and amenities. With an eight boat dock along the navigable
Little Creek, residents can boat into the Chesapeake Bay. Residents can enjoy the serene environment along the
property's pier, or head two blocks north to the shores of Norfolk's Chesapeake Bay beaches. The subject property also
offers in-unit washer and dryer connections, a convenient yet uncommon amenity in the submarket. Over

transaction_notes:
Mariner's Cove Apartments, a 458-unit apartment community located at 832 Blackfriars Drive in Virginia Beach, was sold on May 31, 2018 for $52.15 million.  The property was reported as being in the upper-80% occupancy at sale.  

There were "extensive deferred maintenance and operational issues" reported.  The buyer began renovations shortly after the sale.  The buyer was attracted to the property's location.

The contract period was about 6.5 months, and the buyer obtained financing through Capital One Bank in the amount of $40 million.

The Lafayette, a 168-unit apartment building at 4601 Mayflower Road in Norfolk, was sold on May 8, 2018 for $17 million.  The property has views of the Lafayette River.

The property was about 97% occupied at sale.  There were no conditions reported as having impacted the sale price, although the 1960's style was said to have held the price back somewhat.  The property was marketed with the possibility of adding value through unit interior updates and increasing below market rents.

The cap rate based on the trailing income was reported as about 6.05%.  The net operating income is estimated at $1.028 million after about $250 per unit in reserves.  The buyer reported a going-in cap rate of 6.6%.

The contract period was about 75 days and the buyer obtained financing through CBRE Capital Markets in the amount of $13.68 million.
first_trust_deed_terms: This is a 10 year fixed rate partial interest loan with an interest loan period of 5 years. This loan was used to acquire the property and is based on a 30 year amortizatoin schedule

The property consists of 400 town-home style apartment units in 2-story buildings. The buildings were originally constructed in 1967 and are situated on 26.17 acres. The subject property was gut rehabbed in 2015 in the amount of approximately 15 million dollars. The subject property borders I664, known as Hampton Roads Beltway, a primary traffic artery connecting, Virginia Beach and Norfolk with the balance of the Hampton Roads submarket.  
Meadowview Townhomes consists of 12 one bedroom, one-bathroom units, 186 two-bedroom, one-bathroom units, 190 three-bedroom, one-bathroom units, and 12 four-bedroom, one-and-half -bathroom units. Due to a shortage of larger housing for rent, this unit mix is highly favorable for the area.

transaction_notes:
Geller Associates acquired a 136 unit apartment from Penan & Scott, P.C. on January 5th, 2018.

The former paid $10,725,000 or around $78,860 per unit, at the time of the sale property was fully occupied.  The cap rate based on the first year was reported at about 5.75% after about $200,000 to replace roofs which equates to about 5.85% based on the sale price.  The deal was under escrow for 60 days and the down payment is based on the recorded financing.

The buyer saw the acquisition as a value-add opportunity, the roof will need to be replaced in the near future which will cost $200,000.

The sale price, unit total, cap rate, pro forma cap rate, escrow, motivation, roof repair and occupancy were confirmed by source(s) deemed reliable.
